mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is an open-source alternative to proprietary AI models, designed for self-hosting on consumer-grade hardware. It supports various AI tasks such as text generation, audio, and image processing without requiring a GPU. Ideal for users seeking a local-first, decentralized AI solution.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for interacting with the LocalAI service.

```bash

curl -Lo localai https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai

sudo mv localai /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the LocalAI binary for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This step sets up a Kubernetes cluster where LocalAI will be deployed.

```bash

kind create cluster --name localai --image kindest/node:v1.30.0

```

> Create a local Kubernetes cluster using Kind.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(kind get kubeconfig-path --name=

```

> Set the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to the Kind cluster.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI to the cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI to your Kubernetes cluster using a Helm chart.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://localai.io/charts

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI in the 'localai' namespace.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List the pods in the LocalAI namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall the LocalAI release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
This step outlines the process to upgrade LocalAI to the latest version. Ensure to backup any necessary data before proceeding.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> Drain the LocalAI pods before upgrading.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the LocalAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n localai -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified Docker image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the repository is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI application may be misconfigured or missing required resources.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI service may not be running or is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the LocalAI service is running and check the service configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster cannot pull the Docker image due to authentication issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the image pull secrets are correctly configured if the image is in a private repository.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved API endpoints for better performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n localai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
